mechanism_narrative: A downward turn in FEDFUNDS reduces the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when rate cuts are underway.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if the sign relationship flips for any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above a restrictive threshold, higher funding costs compress equity multiples and raise recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor income and consumption, so an increase in unemployment over the prior quarter predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-ahead unemployment-surprise months are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than half of cases, or if the signal's rolling 3-year IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS growth signals stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth does not coincide with positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relation is negative for any sustained 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO combined with stable employment confirms real activity without a labor-market collapse, supporting cyclical risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and non-declining PAYEMS fails to outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered months yield negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in HOUST and GDPC1 signals broad real-demand acceleration and stronger future cash flows, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with both HOUST and GDPC1 accelerating do not deliver positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than each single-series effect.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is low and payroll growth is still positive, the labor market remains supportive and risk assets benefit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-unemployment and positive-payroll condition does not improve 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if negative returns dominate in triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, stable inflation alongside firm retail sales indicates real-demand resilience without a policy shock, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-and-stable inflation combined with firm sales does not outperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship disappears during disinflation periods.
